slices.focus: Hurricane maps and decompression rhythm with Terrence
slices.summary: Weekend trip to Baytown to look at old hurricane track atlases with friend Terrence turned into a stable decompression habit — sending screenshots after repetitive public meetings to vent without needing technical backstory. Realized concrete artifacts help process stress better than abstract complaining. Decided to thank Terrence directly and start noticing recurring categories in resident concerns across meetings instead of treating each as isolated.
open_loops: Whether to set topic boundaries if family pressure keeps entering the Terrence thread
Whether the meeting explanation approach needs adjusting once recurring patterns are identified
Whether Terrence's response to gratitude reveals his own boundary needs
decisions: Thank Terrence directly for how much the exchange has helped
Start tracking recurring categories of resident doubts across meetings instead of treating each as isolated
Maintain the screenshot plus one concrete sentence format to keep the rhythm light and useful
